channel.title: TeReKG on MIKE Research Clusters
channel.description: Recent content in TeReKG on MIKE Research Clusters

item.title: Version 2.0 Release: Temporal Knowledge Graph (TeReKG) Toolkit
item.description: The MIKE Lab is committed to open science. Version 2.0 of our TeReKG toolkit provides enhanced support for linking GitHub repositories to academic citations, helping software teams maintain architectural consistency over long-term development cycles.— title: “Version 2.0 Release: Temporal Knowledge Graph (TeReKG) Toolkit” date: 2026-02-15 draft: false image: “/images/news/software-release.jpg” teaser: “The lab releases an updated open-source framework for linking scholarly code to technical documentation using contrastive learning.” venue: “Open Source Initiative” categories: [“Software”] tags: [“Systemic Discovery”, “Software Engineering”, “TeReKG”] The MIKE Lab is committed to open science.

item.title: Contrastive Learning for Near-Earth Object Detection in Deep Space Exposures
item.description: Summary of Contribution By applying temporal knowledge graphs (TeReKG) to link sequential exposures, our architecture successfully identified three previously uncatalogued near-Earth objects during our validation phase at the regional observatory.
